title: Guidelines for Verification of the DDNS Method for Poliovirus Detection
author: Joyce Akello

steps.title: STAGE 1 is completed using nuclease...
steps.instruction: STAGE 1 is completed using nuclease free water spiked with the positive control material dilutions. Results of this experiment will show if RNA extraction and PCR amplification work efficiently, establishing detection sensitivity for the viral material in the particular laboratory settings using the equipment available. Using fresh, unfrozen aliquots of the positive control from Step 1 is recommended for STAGE 1 experiment. Prepare ten-fold dilution series (neat to 10 -4 ) of the CVA20 positive control as shown below.

steps.title: Label 3 individual microcentrifuge tubes 10...
steps.instruction: Label 3 individual microcentrifuge tubes 10 -2 to 10 -4 and add 270 µL NFW to each one.

steps.title: Extract the Viral RNA using the...
steps.instruction: Extract the Viral RNA using the MagMAX Viral RNA Isolation kit. Follow steps described here for small volume RNA extraction: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.81wgbzpyogpk/v2 Keep RNA on ice and use fresh to set up RT-PCR reactions. Store left over RNA frozen.

steps.title: Perform the first round RT-PCR on...
steps.instruction: Perform the first round RT-PCR on each RNA extract (same day as RNA extraction) as per DDNS protocol: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rm7vzbyyxvx1/v6 Include PCR negative control by using NFW instead of RNA.

steps.title: Perform barcoded VP1 PCR on all...
steps.instruction: Perform barcoded VP1 PCR on all the first round PCR products as templates and adding an extra PCR negative control, resulting in 13 reactions. Record what barcode is used for each sample.

steps.title: Run all amplicons on agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Run all amplicons on agarose gel or (if available) Tapestation to confirm successful amplification.

steps.title: Store the PCR products in fridge...
steps.instruction: Store the PCR products in fridge for sequencing to be performed alongside amplicons generated in subsequent STAGE 2 and 3.

steps.title: STAGE 2 is designed to show...
steps.instruction: STAGE 2 is designed to show if the assay can be performed efficiently with stool matrix. Individual positive control aliquots are spiked into 5 different, previously tested poliovirus and non-polio enterovirus negative stool suspensions (prepared according to the WHO laboratory protocol). Un-spiked stool samples and regular positive and negative controls complete the sample set for this experiment.

steps.title: Thaw 6 aliquots of the neat...
steps.instruction: Thaw 6 aliquots of the neat positive control solution prepared in Step 1 of this protocol.

steps.title: Spike neat positive control aliquots (30...
steps.instruction: Spike neat positive control aliquots (30 µL) into five tubes with 270 µl of five different negative stool suspensions as shown below and mix gently by pipetting.

steps.title: Extract the Viral RNA using the...
steps.instruction: Extract the Viral RNA using the MagMAX Viral RNA Isolation kit. Follow steps described here for small volume RNA extraction: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.81wgbzpyogpk/v2 Ensure to include the regular positive assay control, negative extraction control and aliquots of the un-spiked stool samples. Keep RNA on ice and use fresh to set up RT-PCR reactions. Store left over RNA frozen.

steps.title: Perform the first round RT-PCR on...
steps.instruction: Perform the first round RT-PCR on each RNA extract (same day of extraction) as per DDNS protocol: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rm7vzbyyxvx1/v6 Include PCR negative control by using NFW instead of RNA.

steps.title: Perform barcoded VP1 PCR on all...
steps.instruction: Perform barcoded VP1 PCR on all the first round PCR products as templates and adding an extra PCR negative control resulting in 14 reactions. Use different barcodes than those used for samples in stage 1. Record accurately what barcode is used for each sample.

steps.title: Run all amplicons on agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Run all amplicons on agarose gel or (if available) Tapestation to confirm successful amplification.

steps.title: Store the amplicons in the fridge...
steps.instruction: Store the amplicons in the fridge or (if available) freeze for sequencing to be performed along aside amplicons generated in STAGE 1 and 3. Individual stool samples might contain PCR inihibiting compounds, resulting in negative result for the spiked sample. In such case, an additional experiment can be set up using 1:10 dilution of the original stool suspension and successful amplification can confirm inhibition in the original spiked sample. A perceived negative stool might come up positive for enterovirus due to sampling effect or viral load around the limit of detection of virus isolation. In such case, an additional experiment will need to be set up with a different negative stool sample.

steps.title: STAGE 3 experiments provide DDNS vs...
steps.instruction: STAGE 3 experiments provide DDNS vs virus isolation and intratypic differentiation method result comparison for a previously characterised set of stool samples.

steps.title: Identify 5 known positive and 5...
steps.instruction: Identify 5 known positive and 5 negative stool suspensions that have been tested by the goldstandard method (virus Isolation and ITD-qPCR) and test them using the DDNS method. We recommend including a mixture of weak and strong positive samples. A negative control consisting of NFW, and the assay positive control (CVA20)must be included in all runs to control for cross-contamination.

steps.title: Perform the first round PCR on...
steps.instruction: Perform the first round PCR on each RNA extract (same day of extraction) dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rm7vzbyyxvx1/v6 Include PCR negative control by using NFW instead of RNA.

steps.title: Perform the nested VP1 on all...
steps.instruction: Perform the nested VP1 on all the first round PCR products Use different barcodes than those used for samples in stage 1 and 2.

steps.title: Run all amplicons on agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Run all amplicons on agarose gel or (if available) Tapestation to confirm successful amplification.

steps.title: Testing of blinded samples will allow...
steps.instruction: Testing of blinded samples will allow the laboratory to test its entire quality system and provide a real time assessment of the laboratory's proficiency in performing DDNS. Stage 4 should be performed after at least 3 – 6 months of ongoing DDNS prospective testing.

steps.title: Undertake library preparation and nanopore sequencing...
steps.instruction: Undertake library preparation and nanopore sequencing according to the DDNS protocol for PCR amplicons generated at this stage, along with amplicons from STAGE 1 and STAGE 2 on by pooling all three sets of barcoded VP1 products.

steps.title: Analyse the generated sequence data using...
steps.instruction: Analyse the generated sequence data using the Piranha GUI software and complete QC checks.

steps.title: DDNS_Verification_Report_V3
steps.instruction: DDNS_Verification_Report_V3.docx Record the results for each stage in the verification report (template attached). Share all documentation along with the report with the PSC team for review.

steps.title: Prepare a worksheet containing a list...
steps.instruction: Prepare a worksheet containing a list of stool samples that have previously been tested for poliovirus by DDNS method. At least 5 to 10 samples containing different combinations of PV, NPEV alone and /or in mixtures including weak and strong positive samples.

steps.title: Assign the sample with a new...
steps.instruction: Assign the sample with a new lab identification number and blind the previous results. Senior scientist overseeing the DDNS testing is responsible for blinding the results and ensuring that lab staff performing the DDNS method receive blinded samples.

steps.title: The blinded samples should go through...
steps.instruction: The blinded samples should go through the entire sample handling and processing workflow as though the samples have just been received in the lab for testing.

steps.title: Subject the samples to the entire DDNS protocol
steps.instruction: Subject the samples to the entire DDNS protocol. dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rm7vzbyyxvx1/v6

steps.title: Analyse the data using the Piranha GUI software
steps.instruction: Analyse the data using the Piranha GUI software.

steps.title: Compare the newly generated results to the previous results
steps.instruction: Compare the newly generated results to the previous results. The newly generated results should match the previously obtained results. If there is a discrepancy in any of the results, it would be regarded as a failed internal quality assurance and the process must be repeated. Occasionally, a sample with viral load close to the limit of detection might swing between positive and negative result on retesting, so results need careful evaluation.

steps.title: DDNS_Verification_Report_V3
steps.instruction: DDNS_Verification_Report_V3.docx Record the results in the verification report (template attached).

steps.title: Share the verification report as well...
steps.instruction: Share the verification report as well as the gel images, run reports and Piranha reports with PSC for review. Once the report is discussed and reviewed, this will become part of your laboratory’s QA records.

steps.title: Working in the MSCII, reconstitute the...
steps.instruction: Working in the MSCII, reconstitute the vial containing the lyophilised CVA20 (freeze dried material) by adding 1mL of Nuclease Free Water (NFW). Control material resuspension ACDP Hazard group 2 infectious material - Handle at Biosafety Level 2 and perform procedures that may generate aerosols in a Class II Microbiological SafetyCabinet (MSCII). No attempt should be made to weigh out or transfer the freeze-dried material.

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting up and...
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting up and down to ensure that the material completely dissolves in water giving a colourless liquid. Check and ensure that any powder on the seal insert or on the wall is also rinsed into the vial.

steps.title: Prepare single use aliquots (30 µL)...
steps.instruction: Prepare single use aliquots (30 µL) in labelled sterile microcentrifuge tubes and keep on ice to use fresh or store at -20 °C until further use. Ensure tube labels have accurate name from vial and preparation date recorded. One vial of lyophilised material should yield ~33 vials of 30 µL reconstituted viral preparations.

steps.title: Extract the Viral RNA using the...
steps.instruction: Extract the Viral RNA using the MagMAX Viral RNA Isolation kit. Follow steps described here : dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.81wgbzpyogpk/v2

steps.title: Mix gently, and add 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Mix gently, and add 30 µL of the prepared 10 -1 dilution to 270 µL water in tube labelled 10 -2 .

steps.title: Use 1 tube of freshly prepared...
steps.instruction: Use 1 tube of freshly prepared aliquot (30 µL) of the reconstituted positive control from Step 1 and add 270 µL NFW. Label this tube as 10 -1 .

steps.title: Mix gently, and add 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Mix gently, and add 30 µL of the prepared 10 -2 dilution to 270 µL water in tube labelled 10 -3 .

steps.title: Mix gently, and add 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Mix gently, and add 30 µL of the prepared 10 -3 dilution to 270 µL water in tube labelled 10 -4 .

steps.title: Prepare negative extraction control (300 µL...
steps.instruction: Prepare negative extraction control (300 µL pure NFW) as sample 11.

steps.title: Spike each ten-fold dilutions in tubes...
steps.instruction: Spike each ten-fold dilutions in tubes 10 -1 to 10 -4 from Step 2 into labelled tubes containing 270 µL of NFW. This should result in 10 prepared samples for viral RNA extraction.

steps.title: Prepare sample series as depicted below
steps.instruction: Prepare sample series as depicted below. STAGE 1 experiment is performed with duplicate samplesyielding two results for each dilution point. Add 270 µL of NFW to two neat 30 µL aliquots from Step 1 and label tubes 1 and 2.

steps.title: Transfer 300 µl of each stool...
steps.instruction: Transfer 300 µl of each stool suspension into separate labelled tubes. (Un-spiked stools, Samples 1-5)

steps.title: Prepare assay controls to complete the sample list
steps.instruction: Prepare assay controls to complete the sample list. Add 270 µl of water to one aliquot of 30 µl positive control - Assay positive control (Sample 11) 300 µl PCR grade water - Negative control (Sample 12) This should result in a total of 12 samples prepared for viral RNA extraction.

steps.title: Share results with PSC team for...
steps.instruction: Share results with PSC team for review before progressing to next stage.

steps.title: Share results with PSC team for...
steps.instruction: Share results with PSC team for review before progressing to next stage.

steps.title: Share results with PSC team for...
steps.instruction: Share results with PSC team for review before progressing to pooling and library preparation.
